thesis.headline: A low-risk design partner pilot for enterprise AI cost governance.
thesis.summary: Argmin can start as a local, read-only, trust-boundary proof that shows where AI is used, what it costs, who owns it, and which workflows have enough evidence to automate.
thesis.design_partner_claim: The product earns a design partner when the buyer sees their own operating model in the demo: finance owns value, platform owns feasibility, security owns trust, and business units see workflow adoption.

readiness_pillars.design_partner_value: Customer teams can evaluate the product story without granting production credentials or waiting for security review.
evidence: Static UI opens from local files or the bundled local server
Smoke test validates health, dashboard, attribution, and intercept paths
Synthetic data is deterministic and safe for screen recording

readiness_pillars.design_partner_value: The demo speaks in the language of enterprise buyers: fail-open, read-only integrations, customer trust boundary, and audit trail.
evidence: Admin diagnostics show read-only source permissions
Governance page separates advisory and active policy modes
Audit log exposes actor, target, outcome, and basis

readiness_pillars.design_partner_value: Product and operations leaders can see where AI is embedded in actual work, not only which models were called.
evidence: 23 workflows mapped across business units
19 AI services and 23 capability classes represented
Insertion points and decision owners are visible

readiness_pillars.design_partner_value: Finance can evaluate whether Argmin is a reporting layer, a control surface, and a planning input.
evidence: Event-time and current-state ownership views remain separate
Unknown and allocation-only rows are explicit
Forecasting keeps scenario assumptions visible

readiness_pillars.design_partner_value: Stakeholders can click through how insight becomes owned work before connecting Slack, Jira, or ServiceNow.
evidence: Eight business workflow routes are modeled
Scenario runner appends local delivery records
Fallback and failed delivery states are visible

pilot_stages.label: 1. Discover
pilot_stages.goal: Map the buyer's AI operating questions to Argmin proof surfaces before requesting any cloud access.
pilot_stages.customer_commitment: Name finance, platform, security, and product owners; provide redacted examples of AI workflows and current pain points.
pilot_stages.argmin_commitment: Configure a customer-shaped local demo narrative and identify which proof surfaces matter for the first meeting.
pilot_stages.success_signal: Stakeholders agree on the first three questions Argmin must answer with their data.

pilot_stages.label: 2. Connect
pilot_stages.goal: Validate read-only ingestion scope and integration feasibility against the customer's actual systems.
pilot_stages.customer_commitment: Review connector scope for billing, identity, SDLC, telemetry, and org hierarchy sources.
pilot_stages.argmin_commitment: Produce a trust-boundary integration plan with explicit read-only permissions and fallback paths.
pilot_stages.success_signal: Security and platform owners agree the initial connector scope is low-risk and measurable.

pilot_stages.label: 3. Prove
pilot_stages.goal: Show request-level attribution, ambiguous ownership handling, chargeback rows, and optimization opportunities on seeded or customer-approved data.
pilot_stages.customer_commitment: Nominate representative AI services, shared service accounts, and business-unit owners for review.
pilot_stages.argmin_commitment: Deliver a replayable proof pack with confidence, evidence class, truth basis, and unresolved gaps.
pilot_stages.success_signal: Finance can explain at least one chargeback-ready line and one intentionally excluded/ambiguous line.

pilot_stages.label: 4. Pilot
pilot_stages.goal: Run a narrow production-adjacent pilot that proves operating value without putting customer AI traffic at risk.
pilot_stages.customer_commitment: Approve success metrics, review cadence, operating owners, and go/no-go criteria.
pilot_stages.argmin_commitment: Support weekly operating review with savings, coverage, adoption, governance, and integration handoff evidence.
pilot_stages.success_signal: The buyer asks to connect the next business unit, provider, or workflow because the value is operationally clear.

buyer_roles.name: Budget owner
buyer_roles.cares_about: Can AI spend be attributed, allocated, forecasted, and defended without another spreadsheet reconciliation cycle?

buyer_roles.name: AI platform owner
buyer_roles.cares_about: Can the product see actual runtime behavior and route interventions into implementation work without slowing developers?

buyer_roles.name: Trust-boundary owner
buyer_roles.cares_about: Can governance, audit, and policy simulation happen without exfiltrating customer data or blocking AI traffic?

buyer_roles.name: Adoption owner
buyer_roles.cares_about: Which teams are embedding AI into real workflows, where is adoption stalling, and what should be scaled next?

proof_moments.moment: Show workflow-level adoption across product, support, finance, security, and data teams.
proof_moments.why_it_converts: Answers exactly where AI is entering work, not just who has an AI bill.

proof_moments.moment: Replay one high-confidence request and one ambiguous ownership case.
proof_moments.why_it_converts: Makes the attribution chain tangible and shows the product is honest about uncertainty.

proof_moments.moment: Open event-time chargeback rows and unknown-spend exclusions.
proof_moments.why_it_converts: Demonstrates accounting discipline and avoids overclaiming allocation quality.

proof_moments.moment: Compare baseline, growth, hardened-policy, and optimization-capture forecast paths.
proof_moments.why_it_converts: Shows Argmin as an operating-planning surface rather than a month-end reporting layer.

proof_moments.moment: Show energy ratings and preserve unrated/unknown models instead of inventing precision.
proof_moments.why_it_converts: Builds trust by making energy visible while keeping cost, quality, and policy context intact.

proof_moments.moment: Show fail-open behavior, read-only integration posture, and audit log.
proof_moments.why_it_converts: Frames Argmin as an in-boundary operating layer rather than a risky external observer.

proof_moments.moment: Run an integration scenario so insight becomes a routed business handoff.
proof_moments.why_it_converts: Makes the product feel operational instead of report-only.

success_metrics.target: All top-spend ambiguous workloads triaged within two business days
Top 10 customer workflows mapped to team, tool, owner, and capability
Three accepted recommendations converted into implementation tickets

success_metrics.baseline: Read-only posture visible in local demo
success_metrics.target: Security signs off initial connector scope and audit boundaries
Pilot sponsor uses Argmin forecast in one operating review

open_questions.question: Which spend should be charged back versus reported as shared platform allocation?

open_questions.question: What evidence can leave the customer boundary, if any?
open_questions.how_demo_handles_it: Admin and Governance keep trust-boundary copy visible and position production access as customer-controlled.

open_questions.question: Which workflows are strategically important enough to map first?
open_questions.how_demo_handles_it: Employee Adoption exposes business-unit workflows, insertion points, decision owners, and adoption quality.

demo_script.title: Start with the buyer's operating question
demo_script.talk_track: AI usage is already inside workflows; the problem is that ownership, cost, and adoption are fragmented.

demo_script.title: Show adoption as workflow intelligence
demo_script.talk_track: The demo identifies where teams use AI services and how those services fit into business-unit work.

demo_script.title: Prove request-level truth
demo_script.talk_track: One request carries evidence, confidence, cost basis, and owner lineage; ambiguous cases stay explicit.

demo_script.title: Make the trust boundary visible
demo_script.talk_track: Read-only integration posture, fail-open behavior, and audit events are first-class parts of the product story.

demo_script.title: Close with the pilot ask
demo_script.talk_track: The next step is not vague feedback; it is a scoped design-partner pilot with owners and success metrics.
